title: BCIT Mind Wandering
description: The BCIT Mind Wandering dataset comprises electroencephalography (EEG), eye tracking, and vehicle performance data collected during prolonged simulated driving tasks designed to assess fatigue-related biomarkers and driver performance under different cognitive load conditions. Participants performed three counterbalanced 30-minute driving sessions with different background audio conditions (task-relevant traffic safety, non-task-relevant sports broadcast, and internal focus mindfulness breathing) while responding to vigilance targets (police vehicle identification) and managing vehicle perturbations. This dataset extends prior driving research by incorporating extended time-on-task measurements and novel audio manipulations to investigate mind wandering and fatigue-induced performance decrements.
methods_description: Participants performed a calibration driving task (15 minutes) followed by three counterbalanced Mind Wandering conditions (30 minutes each). EEG was recorded using BioSemi 64-channel systems (plus 8 additional channels including eye and mastoid references) at 2048 Hz. Eye tracking was performed using Sensomotoric Instruments REDEYE250. Vehicle data were logged at 100 Hz from a driving simulator with steering wheel and pedal controls. Participants maintained lane position while responding to lateral vehicle perturbations and identifying police vehicles (vigilance task). Background audio conditions varied across sessions: task-relevant (traffic safety), non-task-relevant (sports broadcast), and internal focus (mindfulness breathing). Subjective fatigue was assessed using Task-Induced Fatigue Scale (TIFS), Karolinska Sleepiness Scale (KSS), and Visual Analog Scale of Fatigue (VAS-F).
